summary: Pro-state-flicker bug fix VERIFIED end-to-end. Backend: 4/4 pro_state regression tests pass (test_pro_state_preservation.py — upgrade flips is_pro, increment preserves is_pro via $setOnInsert, portfolio/save doesn't touch scan_counters, full journey from free→limit→upgrade→save→scan keeps is_pro=true). Frontend: with persisted pv.isPro.v1='1' in AsyncStorage (localStorage on web), the scan tab's bottom badge reads 'PRO · Unlimited' at t~50ms on EVERY entry (initial nav, hard reload, dash→scan refocus, post-delete refocus) — ZERO instances of 'Free scans left' flicker observed across 4 navigation scenarios. CRITICAL ASSERTION (zero-network Pro gate): tapping the shutter on the scan tab as a Pro user produced ZERO /api/scan/count calls (network log empty) — proStore.get() synchronously short-circuits the gate in capture() at scan.tsx:104. PERSISTENCE: hard browser reload while on /scan shows 'PRO · Unlimited' as soon as the scan-counter element mounts (~150ms), proving AsyncStorage hydration via proStore.hydrate() lands before the badge first paints; the 3 follow-up GET /scan/count calls are upgrade-only syncs and cannot downgrade. LOGOUT RESET: clicking logout-btn clears localStorage 'pv.isPro.v1' from '1' to null — proStore.reset() works. ISOLATION (static review): grep across /app/frontend for proStore/useIsPro returns ZERO references in /app/frontend/app/card-detail.tsx and /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/dashboard.tsx — save/delete flows cannot mutate Pro state. Mock paywall upgrade end-to-end: /paywall → subscribe → POST /api/scan/upgrade → proStore.setPro(true) BEFORE router.replace → dashboard renders without paywall reappearing. Pre-existing non-blocking warnings carry over (textShadow*/shadow*/pointerEvents deprecations on RN-web; iter7's dashboard?deletedId first-mount setParams crash also still reproducible but unrelated to this iteration). No new bugs found.

react_native_issues.component: RN-web deprecated style props (pre-existing, carryover from iter5-7)
react_native_issues.issue: shadow*/textShadow* style props warn on first paint on RN-web. Migrate to single 'boxShadow' / 'textShadow' string props to silence. Non-blocking. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/dashboard.tsx:466-470 (totalValue textShadow*), /app/frontend/app/card-detail.tsx:446,459 (estimateValue textShadow*, retentionMarker shadow*), /app/frontend/app/paywall.tsx:296-298,336-338,365-368 (saveBadge/priceValueActive/cta shadow*+textShadow*).

RN-web warns 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Source likely in scan.tsx mask View at scan.tsx:211 and frame View at scan.tsx:214 (pointerEvents='none' prop usage). Move to style={{ pointerEvents: 'none' }}. Non-blocking.

react_native_issues.component: dashboard.tsx deletedId first-mount crash (carryover from iter7 — NOT fixed yet)
react_native_issues.issue: Reloading or deep-linking /dashboard?deletedId=X still throws 'Attempted to navigate before mounting the Root Layout component' from the router.setParams call at /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/dashboard.tsx:70. Iteration 7 recommended deferring with setTimeout/queueMicrotask. Current code uses queueMicrotask which still races on web first-mount. Not in scope of the Pro-state fix but still open.

action_items: (Optional, low-priority follow-up) Silence the RN-web deprecation warnings: convert shadow*/textShadow*/pointerEvents prop usages to their new style-object equivalents in dashboard.tsx, card-detail.tsx, paywall.tsx, and scan.tsx (mask/frame pointerEvents props).

(Carryover from iter7, still open) Fix the router.setParams first-mount crash at dashboard.tsx:70 — queueMicrotask is still racing on web. A safer pattern is a useRef gate that fires the setParams only after a second render tick, or window.history.replaceState fallback on web.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/pro-store.ts — clean module-level singleton: synchronous get(), idempotent setPro() guards against listener spam (line 55 `_isPro === value && _hydrated` early-return), hydrate() is single-shot, reset() persists removal to AsyncStorage. Subscribe pattern is correct. useIsPro() initialises state from proStore.get() on first render — no false-flash possible (line 102).

/app/frontend/src/auth-context.tsx:19-21 — proStore.hydrate() fires in a useEffect on mount of AuthProvider. This means hydration is async (AsyncStorage roundtrip). On web (localStorage) this is fast (<1 frame) but on native it can take 10-30ms. Minor concern: useIsPro() reads proStore.get() synchronously on first render which returns the module-level default `false` if mount happens before the hydrate await resolves. Not observed in practice on web (the badge consistently shows 'PRO · Unlimited' at t~50ms), but worth flagging for native.

/app/frontend/src/auth-context.tsx:33-39 — login path is correctly UPGRADE-ONLY (`if (c.is_pro) proStore.setPro(true);`), and a logout calls proStore.reset(). Confirmed by localStorage transition '1' → null on logout-btn tap.
/app/frontend/app/(tabs)/scan.tsx:36-50 — useIsPro() drives the badge; useEffect at 46-50 mirrors RC entitlement into the store; isPro at line 40 is sticky via OR composition. All correct.

/app/frontend/app/(tabs)/scan.tsx:96-120 — capture() reads proStore.get() synchronously at line 104. VERIFIED: ZERO /api/scan/count calls fired when shutter is tapped as Pro user. Free-user path correctly fetches count, upgrades store if backend says is_pro, otherwise gates at count>=free_limit.
/app/frontend/app/(tabs)/scan.tsx:56-70 — useFocusEffect refetches scan/count on every focus but only calls proStore.setPro(true) when c.is_pro is true (line 64). Cannot downgrade. CORRECT.

/app/frontend/app/paywall.tsx:50-77 — both RC real-purchase path (line 51-55) and mock fallback (line 71-75) call proStore.setPro(true) BEFORE router.replace. Restore path at lines 88-104 also sets store on confirmed entitlement. All four call sites correct.
/app/frontend/app/card-detail.tsx + dashboard.tsx — grep confirms ZERO proStore/useIsPro references. Save and delete flows cannot mutate Pro state by design.

Backend (test_pro_state_preservation.py) — 4/4 PASS: upgrade flips is_pro=true; increment uses $setOnInsert and preserves is_pro across 4 subsequent /scan/count POSTs; portfolio/save does not touch scan_counters collection (verified via before/after GET); full journey free→exhaust→upgrade→save→scan keeps is_pro=true throughout.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on testable Pro-flicker assertions: PRO badge at first paint on initial scan nav, hard reload on /scan, dashboard→scan refocus, post-delete refocus; ZERO /api/scan/count calls on shutter tap as Pro user; localStorage clears on logout. 1 carry-over RN Alert.alert limitation (cannot E2E-test card-detail trash on web) and 1 carry-over deletedId first-mount crash, both pre-existing.

seed_data_creation: Reused iter7's 3 saved cards (Mewtwo/Charizard/Pikachu) for uid ZhopI1m5jeQJr5MBoZbHPXqAFtH3. Created 5 TEST_* scan_counters during pytest run (uids prefixed TEST_pro_*, TEST_save_pro_*, TEST_journey_*) — these are isolated, never collide with the production user, and the save_card/journey tests clean up their portfolio rows via DELETE in the test body. scan_counters rows themselves are not deleted; they are negligible and safe.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pro-store implementation is solid. If you need to re-test: (1) Camera permission must be granted via context.grant_permissions(['camera'], origin=...) so the scan-screen renders (not the permission gate). (2) Use page.evaluate to read window.localStorage.getItem('pv.isPro.v1') as the AsyncStorage equivalent on web. (3) Tap the shutter via [data-testid='scan-shutter'] and assert ZERO /api/scan/count network calls — that proves the synchronous Pro-gate. (4) Mock-upgrade path is /paywall → [data-testid='paywall-subscribe-button']. (5) Carryover open bugs: dashboard?deletedId=X first-mount crash (iter7 RCA), shadow*/textShadow*/pointerEvents RN-web deprecation warnings. None of these regress the Pro-state contract.
